violations.text: Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink at the bar. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/23/2025

Raw shelled eggs were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods such as salami in the walk-in refrigerator in basement. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/23/2025
The bar area has an accumulation of old rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 12/26/2025

The ceiling tiles in the basement food prep area are constructed of composite acoustic tile which is not durable, easily cleanable, or non-absorbent. The materials for ceilings must be durable, easily cleanable and nonabsorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, and ware-washing areas. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2025
No person certified in food safety was present as needed during time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2025

Employees were observed eating in kitchen at food prep tables. An employee shall eat, drink, or use any form of tobacco only in designated areas where the contamination of exposed food, clean equipment, utensils, food contact surfaces and other items needing protection can not result. PIC informed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2025
Several handsinks in the facility are lacking notification signage. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination (raw shelled eggs over raw fish). Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2025

Per interview of staff, the containers used for storing cut meat are only washed with soap and water. After being cleaned, equipment and utensils must be sanitized in the 3-bay-sink or dishwasher. PIC informed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2025
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. New Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Large stockpots of soup were placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Foods were assessed and placed into shallower containers to continue cooling process during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2025
Automatic soap dispenser at basement handsink was not operational. Equipment to be in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine in the kitchen. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025
The door to the toilet room in the kitchen is not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025

The ceiling tiles in the basement food prep area are constructed of composite acoustic tile which is not durable, easily cleanable, or non-absorbent. The materials for ceilings must be durable, easily cleanable and nonabsorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, and ware-washing areas. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2025

Employees' keys and other personal belongings were observed stored on top of the food contact surfaces of single service items. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. Container was voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2025
